name: Enchiladas Banderas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, boil chicken in water to cover until tender; reserve 2 cups broth.
2. Debone and dice the chicken.
3. Add chicken, tomatoes, onion, salt, and garlic to the reserved broth. Simmer for 10 minutes or until reduced enough for enchilada filling. Strain.
4. Heat a bit of oil in a small skillet; press each tortilla into the hot oil for a few seconds to soften them and make them more pliable for rolling.
5. Stuff filled tortillas with chicken filling.
6. Roll filled tortillas and place in a baking dish.
7. Cover one-third of the enchiladas (longways) with salsa verde, the center third with sour cream, and the final third with salsa roja. The result should resemble the Mexican flag.
8. Bake at 375 degrees until thoroughly heated, about 25 minutes.
Salsa Colorado:
1. Wash chiles, remove stems and seeds. Cook in boiling water to cover for 10 minutes or until soft. Remove and drain.
2. Reserve liquid. Place chiles in a blender with reserved water and whirl until you achieve a pasty consistency.
3. Heat oil in a large skillet. Add garlic and flour and cook, stirring until the flour browns.
4. Gradually add chile paste to mix well with flour into a smooth paste. When all the chile paste is added, bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
5. Cook until thickened, 3-4 minutes. Thin with water to a thick, saucy consistency.
Salsa Verde:
1. Boil tomatillos in a small amount of water until tender; blend in a food processor.
2. In a skillet, sauté onions in oil. Add pureed tomatillos, salt, and garlic.
